back_translation: For the choirmaster. According to Sheminith. A psalm of David.
Help, O LORD, for the godly one is gone; the faithful have vanished from among the sons of men.
Everyone speaks falsehood to his neighbor; with flattering lips and a double heart they speak.
May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that speaks boastfully,
those who say, 'With our tongue we will prevail; our lips are our own—who is master over us?'
The LORD says, 'Because of the oppression of the poor and the groaning of the needy, now I will arise; I will set him in the safety he longs for.'
The words of the LORD are pure words, like silver refined in a furnace, purified seven times.
You, O LORD, will protect us; You will guard us from this generation forever.
The wicked strut about on every side, when vileness is exalted among the sons of men.
